title: 2026 Golden Globes Predictions from PureWow Entertainment Editor
text: Philip Mutz, VP of News and Entertainment at PureWow, has shared his predictions for the 83rd annual Golden Globes ceremony airing January 11, 2026 on CBS and Paramount+. Nikki Glaser returns as host. Mutz predicts Sinners will win Best Motion Picture, Drama. He describes it as his favorite 2025 film, directed by Ryan Coogler and starring Michael B. Jordan: "The movie is scary, sexy, thrilling, hilarious and littered" with deeper meaning. For Best Actor in Drama, Mutz forecasts Michael B. Jordan will triumph in the specific category, though acknowledges stiff Oscar competition from Timothée Chalamet. Jessie Buckley is predicted to win Best Actress in Drama for Hamnet. The editor calls this prediction "one of the easiest categories" to forecast. One Battle After Another is predicted to claim Best Motion Picture, Comedy or Musical, having recently won Best Picture at the Critics' Choice Awards. For television, Mutz predicts Best Limited Series will go to Adolescence (described as "a tough, but important watch"), Best Drama Series will go to The Pitt, and Best Comedy Series will go to The Studio (featuring Seth Rogen, Bryan Cranston, and Catherine O'Hara). Jean Smart is predicted to win Best Actress in Comedy for Hacks (repeating last year's win).
